title: One Rusted Chain
central_metaphor: The broken porch swing as a pendulum, measuring not just time passed but the emotional distance and unspoken love left behind in childhood.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The gravel drive is overgrown and gray
That old tin mailbox leans the other way
I hear the screen door slamming in my mind
A sound I left a thousand miles behind
[Pre-Chorus]
The paint is peeling like a sunburned cheek
The whole place whispers something it can’t speak
[Chorus]
That old front porch swing’s empty in the rain
Still keeping time on one rusted chain
A pendulum for memories that stay
Measuring the distance from today
[Verse 2]
I see the ghost of daddy’s overalls
A shadow stretching long across the walls
He’s hanging up the hose, the job all done
And I’m the little foreman in the sun
[Pre-Chorus]
The paint is peeling like a sunburned cheek
The whole place whispers something it can’t speak
[Chorus]
That old front porch swing’s empty in the rain
Still keeping time on one rusted chain
A pendulum for memories that stay
Measuring the distance from today
[Bridge]
Now my own key turns in a polished lock
On a quiet street where the sidewalks talk
But I’d trade every silent, settled thing
To hear that one loose board start to sing
[Final Chorus]
That old front porch swing’s empty in the rain
Still keeping time on one rusted chain
A pendulum for memories that stay
**Measuring the love I gave away**
